title: Bruins’ breakout prospect Cooper Simpson has a clear path to follow in Will Zellers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Cooper Simpson and Will Zellers, both Bruins prospects from Minnesota, are heading to North Dakota for their first season together.
Simpson was a third-round pick in the 2025 NHL Draft, known for scoring 34 goals and 74 points with Youngstown Phantoms.
Zellers, a former USHL Player of the Year, was acquired in a trade and is recognized for his potent scoring ability.

title: New York woman indicted in alleged sex trafficking operation out of apartments in Allston, Brighton
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Zengzeng Liu, 40, of New York, indicted for running sex trafficking out of Allston and Brighton brothels.
Liu recruited foreign women for commercial sex acts in Boston and controlled appointments and earnings.
Federal agents found $105,000 in cash and evidence of sex trafficking in Allston and Brighton apartments.

title: ‘As a former Celtic, I do not approve’: Basketball world reacts to the Jaylen Brown trade
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to the 76ers for Paul George and four draft picks, sparking strong reactions.
Former Celtic Kendrick Perkins disapproved of the trade, while Quentin Richardson saw the return as adequate.
Local and national media criticized the trade, questioning the Celtics' improvements and strategy under Brad Stevens.

title: A conversation with David Ortiz about the 2004 Red Sox, life as a baseball dad, and the current state of the team
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: David Ortiz reminisces about the Red Sox's 2004 World Series win, highlighting the team's chemistry and support.
Ortiz reflects on his role supporting younger teammates during the Red Sox's 2013 championship season.
Ortiz expresses optimism about the future of the Red Sox, urging fans to remain patient for success.

title: A 1700s Colonial reproduction in Western Massachusetts — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: A 3-bedroom, 1700s Colonial-style reproduction home in Worthington was built in 1976.
The home features 1,889 square feet of space with 3 bathrooms and private sewer/water systems.
Property taxes for the house were $8,011 in 2025, with potential list prices of $579K, $589K, or $599K.

title: UMass University Campus nurses vote to authorize 14-day strike
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: UMass Memorial Medical Center's University Campus nurses voted 1,233 to 17 to authorize a 14-day strike.
The strike aims to advocate for safer patient care and improved recruitment and retention of experienced nurses.
The strike authorization allows the bargaining committee to initiate the strike if negotiations with hospital executives fail.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (July 2, 2026)
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: The Alley, a new 6,500-square-foot food hall at One Post Office Square, opened with multiple dining concepts.
Flann O'Brien's Irish bar, closed during the pandemic, is reopening in Mission Hill by the end of 2026.
America's oldest candy company, Ye Olde Pepper Candy Companie, launched a popup at Old North Church, open till Halloween.

title: Mass. woman, 20, arrested after her Tesla hit 120 mph in Connecticut, police say
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: A Massachusetts woman was arrested for driving a Tesla at 120 mph on I-395 in Connecticut.
The 20-year-old from Framingham was charged with reckless driving and passing on the right.
She was released on $1,000 bond and is set to appear in court on July 7.

title: Fairhaven man charged with first-degree murder after fatal shooting, police chase
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Nicholas Bottella, 33, from Fairhaven, charged with first-degree murder following a fatal shooting in Mattapoisett.
James Rizza, 57, died from multiple gunshot wounds; incident followed a neighbor dispute from the previous year.
Bottella led police on a chase through Marion; pleaded not guilty and is held without bail pending July 31 hearing.

title: Brockton woman identified as Lakeville drowning victim
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Daisha Murphy, 25, from Brockton, drowned after falling off a jet ski on Long Pond in Lakeville.
Murphy was not wearing a life jacket when she fell off a jet ski with another rider.
Her body was discovered by the Southeastern Massachusetts Law Enforcement Council's marine unit around 12:40 a.m. on Wednesday.
